clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (WBC 14.2 K/uL) and evidence of acute kidney injury (AKI Stage 3) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for sepsis risk. Thrombocytopenia (88 K/uL) with progressive decline and bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) suggest hepatic dysfunction. Hemoglobin at 9.8 g/dL indicates anemia, potentially contributing to tissue hypoxia.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2
prioritized_risks.threshold: Suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2

prioritized_risks.explanation: AKI Stage 3 confirmed by creatinine 8.9 mg/dL (≥3x baseline) per KDIGO criteria
Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulation score criteria for coagulopathy
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor requirements and hypotension data unavailable
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent hypotension + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per local sepsis protocol
Order blood cultures and infection-focused imaging
Start vasopressors if hypotension develops despite fluid resuscitation
Monitor platelet trends and consider transfusion if <50 K/uL
Assess renal replacement therapy eligibility for AKI Stage 3
doctor_handoff: Review for source of infection (focus on abdominal/pulmonary sources given bilirubin elevation and leukocytosis). Confirm vasopressor requirements and central venous pressure. Reassess creatinine trends once outlier-excluded values are available.

disease_progression.observation: Baseline abnormalities: WBC 14.2 K/uL, hemoglobin 9.8 g/dL, bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.

family_communication.english_summary: In the last 12 hours, we've seen signs that your loved one's body is fighting a serious infection (sepsis risk), their kidneys are struggling (Stage 3 kidney injury), and their blood is having trouble clotting properly (low platelets). The team has started strong antibiotics, is testing for infection sources, and closely monitoring kidney function. We're also watching for signs their blood pressure might need extra support. The care plan includes finding the infection source, protecting the kidneys, and ensuring safe blood clotting.
family_communication.regional_summary: पिछले 12 घंटों में हमें लगता है कि आपके प्रिय के शरीर में गंभीर संक्रमण है (सिप्सिस जोखिम), गुर्दे काम नहीं कर रहे हैं (तीसरे चरण की चोट), और उनके रक्त थक्का बनाने में समस्या है (कम प्लेटलेट्स)। टीम मजबूत एंटीबायोटिक्स शुरू कर चुकी है, संक्रमण के स्रोत की जांच कर रही है, और गुर्दे के कामकाज की निगरानी कर रही है। हम इस बात की भी निगरानी कर रहे हैं कि क्या उनके रक्तचाप को अतिरिक्त समर्थन की आवश्यकता हो सकती है। देखभाल योजना में संक्रमण के स्रोत का पता लगाना, गुर्दों की रक्षा करना और सुरक्षित रक्त थक्का बनाने की निगरानी शामिल है।
